Opcode.name: PCM_OP_CUST_SET_BAL_GRP
Opcode.Doc: This is a wrapper opcode for the PCM_OP_CUST_CREATE_BAL_GRP and PCM_OP_CUST_MODIFY_BAL_GRP opcodes. If balance monitoring is enabled, it will create or update the /balance_group/monitor object. This opcode can also set the credit limits and consumption rule.

Poid.name: PIN_FLD_POID
Poid.Doc: Routing poid. Only DB is significant. Could be any poid, but it is usually an account poid.

String.name: PIN_FLD_PROGRAM_NAME
String.Doc: Program name. Identifies the calling program.

Timestamp.name: PIN_FLD_START_T
Timestamp.Doc: Start time. Timestamp of when event started (system time).

Timestamp.name: PIN_FLD_END_T
Timestamp.Doc: End time. Timestamp of when event ended (system time). This field is used to determine which billing cycle the event is part of.

Array.name: PIN_FLD_BAL_INFO
Array.Doc: Array of balance group fields. There can only be one element. Element-id is not significant.

Poid.name: PIN_FLD_POID
Poid.Doc: Balance group poid. Type-only poid if the object is being created, otherwise complete poid is needed if the object is being modified (and BILLINFO_OBJ needs to be passed in).

Poid.name: PIN_FLD_ACCOUNT_OBJ
Poid.Doc: Account poid this balane group belongs to.

Poid.name: PIN_FLD_SERVICE_OBJ
Poid.Doc: Service object for this new balane group. A NULL poid means this balance group will be mapped to the account level, otherwise, this balance group will be mapped to this service instance.

Poid.name: PIN_FLD_BILLINFO_OBJ
Poid.Doc: Billinfo object for this balance group. Optional if the balance group is being created, else mandatory if the balance group is being modified.

String.name: PIN_FLD_NAME
String.Doc: Balance group name. Optional if the balance group is being created, else mandatory if the balance group is being modified.

String.name: PIN_FLD_TYPE_STR
String.Doc: Group sharing monitor type. Payment Responsible Real-Time Credit Enforcement group will be created automatically if the monitor type is PR_RTCE.

Array.name: PIN_FLD_LIMIT
Array.Doc: Array of credit limits for each resource in the balance group. More than one element can appear in this array. The element-id matches the balance element whose limit is to be set.

Decimal.name: PIN_FLD_CREDIT_LIMIT
Decimal.Doc: Limit for this resource. Current balance cannot exceed this limit unless affected by a limit override rate, or manually adjusted by a CSR. By default, the limit is set to 0.

Decimal.name: PIN_FLD_CREDIT_FLOOR
Decimal.Doc: Baseline for calculating percentage for threshold triggers. Credit limit minus credit floor is 100% of measured range. Credit floor does NOT enforce any limit on current balance.

Int.name: PIN_FLD_DYNAMIC_CREDIT_FLOOR
Int.Doc: Flags to indicate dynamic credit floor setting, whether to use credit_floor or sum of original balances for credit thresholds check

Int.name: PIN_FLD_OVERRIDE_CREDIT_LIMIT
Int.Doc: Flag to determine to either consider or not consider the credit limit. If flag is 0,(which is default), then credit limit will be decided based on rating modes. If the flag is 0x01, then credit limit will be overriden.

Int.name: PIN_FLD_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS
Int.Doc: Bit flags for triggering when resource balance crosses a boundary when increasing in value.

Decimal.name: PIN_FLD_AMOUNT
Decimal.Doc: Fixed threshold value in Decimal data type

Array.name: PIN_FLD_RULES
Array.Doc: Consumption Rule array. Array of consumption rules for each currency resource in the balance group. The element-id is the resource Id for which this consumption rule applies.

Enum.name: PIN_FLD_CONSUMPTION_RULE
Enum.Doc: Consumption rule to be applied on the resource. Possible values are: (1) EST, (2) LST, (3) EET, (4) LET, (5) ESTLET, (6) ESTEET, (7) LSTEET, (8) LSTLET, (9) EETEST, (10) EETLST, (11) LETEST, and (12) LETLST.

Values.Doc: The consumption rule is not defined.
Earliest start time. Consume the sub-balance with the earliest validity start time (earliest valid_from) first.
Latest start time. Consume the sub-balance with the latest validity start time (latest valid_from) first.
Earliest expiration time. Consume the sub-balance that expires first (earliest valid_to.)
Latest expiration time. Consume the sub-balance that expires last (latest valid_to.)

Earliest start time and latest expiration time. Consume the sub-balance with the earliest valid_from first. If more than one sub-balances has the same valid_from, consume the one with the latest valid_to first.

Earliest start time and earliest expiration time. Consume the sub-balance with the earliest valid_from first. If more than one sub-balances has the same valid_from, consume the one with the earliest valid_to first.

Latest start time and earliest expiration time. Consume the sub-balance with the latest sub-balance first. If more than one sub-balances has the same valid_from, consume the one with the earliest expired sub-balance first.

Latest start time and latest expiration time. Consume the sub-balance with the latest valid_from first. If more than one sub-balances has the same valid_from, consume the one with the latest valid_to first.

Earliest expiration time and earliest start time. Consume the sub-balance with the earliest valid_to first. If more than one sub-balances has the same valid_to, consume the one with the earliest valid_from first.

Earliest expiration time and latest start time. Consume the sub-balance with the earliest valid_to first. If more than one sub-balances has the same valid_to, consume the one with the latest valid_from first.

Latest expiration time and earliest start time. Consume the sub-balance with the latest valid_to first. If more than one sub-balances has the same valid_to, use the one with the earliest valid_from first.

Latest expiration time and latest start time. Consume the sub-balance with the latest valid_to first. If more than one sub-balances has the same valid_to, use the one with the latest valid_from first.

Substruct.name: PIN_FLD_CONTEXT_INFO
Substruct.Doc: Substruct to pass information from external clients.

String.name: PIN_FLD_CORRELATION_ID
String.Doc: Correlation id passed by an external system to BRM.

String.name: PIN_FLD_EXTERNAL_USER
String.Doc: User of an external system connecting to BRM.

Timestamp.name: PIN_FLD_VALID_FROM
Timestamp.Doc: Start date of this validity. If unit and offset are also passed PIN_FLD_VALID_FROM takes precedence

Enum.name: PIN_FLD_VALID_FROM_UNIT
Enum.Doc:
Used for calculating validity if PIN_FLD_VALID_FROM is not passed. Relative Unit for validity start date can be
Seconds(1)
Minutes(2)
Hours(3)
Days(4)
Months(5)
Act Cycles(8)

Values.Doc: Seconds
Minutes
Hours
Days
Months
Act Cycles

Int.name: PIN_FLD_VALID_FROM_OFFSET
Int.Doc: Used for calculating validity if PIN_FLD_VALID_FROM is not passed. Relative offset for validity to be used in conjunction with unit to calculate start time

Timestamp.name: PIN_FLD_VALID_TO
Timestamp.Doc: End date of this validity. If unit and offset are also passed PIN_FLD_VALID_TO takes precedence

Enum.name: PIN_FLD_VALID_TO_UNIT
Enum.Doc:
Used for calculating validity if PIN_FLD_VALID_TO is not passed. Relative Unit for validity end date can be
Seconds(1)
Minutes(2)
Hours(3)
Days(4)
Months(5)
Act Cycles(8)

Values.Doc: Seconds
Minutes
Hours
Days
Months
Act Cycles

Int.name: PIN_FLD_VALID_TO_OFFSET
Int.Doc: Used for calculating validity if PIN_FLD_VALID_TO is not passed. Relative offset for validity to be used in conjunction with unit to calculate end time

Int.name: PIN_FLD_REC_ID
Int.Doc: RecId for the sub-balance to be updated

Substruct.name: PIN_FLD_CONTEXT_INFO
Substruct.Doc: Substruct to pass information from external clients.

String.name: PIN_FLD_CORRELATION_ID
String.Doc: Correlation id passed by an external system to BRM.

String.name: PIN_FLD_EXTERNAL_USER
String.Doc: User of an external system connecting to BRM.
